domain: Aboriginal Australian / First Nations ecological practice (fire-stick farming / cool burns)
specific_mechanism: Indigenous Australian fire-stick farming and First Nations cool-burn practices: low-intensity small-area mosaic-pattern fires conducted in cool dry seasons to reduce fuel load, encourage specific food plants, maintain habitat heterogeneity, and manage wildlife. The pattern is deliberately mosaic (patches of different ages co-exist), low-intensity (does not crown trees), scheduled (specific season-windows), and outcome-targeted (specific plant species are encouraged or suppressed). Practiced 60,000+ years; contrasts with Western fire-suppression which allows fuel accumulation leading to catastrophic crown fires.
llm_application: Proactive small-scale 'cool-burn' discipline for long-running LLM agent runtime: deliberately trigger small, scheduled, mosaic-pattern controlled failures across the agent's operational state (memory eviction batches, prompt-template reset, retrieval-cache flush) to prevent cumulative buildup that would otherwise lead to catastrophic failure. Distinguished from chaos engineering by being mosaic-and-cool (not random and not aggressive) and outcome-targeted (specific kinds of state are targeted).
novelty_claim: Chaos Engineering for LLM-MAS (arxiv 2505.03096) directly applies chaos engineering to LLM multi-agent systems. ChaosEater (2511.07865) automates chaos engineering with LLMs. The proactive-failure-injection-for-LLM-agents space is occupied. AI+indigenous-fire literature uses indigenous knowledge to inform AI for real fire management (USC, ForestNews, Tandfonline 17567505); the reverse direction. The Aboriginal-cool-burn-as-LLM-runtime-discipline framing isn't directly imported, but the underlying proactive-failure-injection-for-resilience mechanism IS published.
